groups.name: Community
groups.explanation: Greendale (the community college location), Abed Nadir (character), Joel McHale (actor who plays Jeff Winger), and 'Six Seasons and a Movie' (the fan rallying cry / eventual reality) are all from Community.

groups.name: Stranger Things
groups.explanation: Hawkins (the town setting), Starcourt Mall (iconic location), Eleven (character/alias), and 'Friends Don't Lie' (Eleven's recurring quote) are all from Stranger Things. The trap: Eleven looks like it could be a 'contains_number' tile, and Hawkins/Starcourt look like fictional towns — but all four belong together.

groups.name: Fictional TV Towns
groups.explanation: Dillon (Friday Night Lights), Rosewood (Pretty Little Liars), Stars Hollow (Gilmore Girls), and Pawnee (Parks and Recreation) are all iconic fictional small towns from different TV shows. The trap: Hawkins and Greendale also sound like fictional towns, but those belong to their respective show groups.

groups.name: Characters Who Aren't Who They Say They Are
groups.explanation: Saul Goodman is the alias of Jimmy McGill (Better Call Saul); Keyser Söze is the secret identity of Verbal Kint in The Usual Suspects (adapted as a TV reference in crime dramas); Boo Radley is the misunderstood recluse whose true gentle identity is hidden from the town in To Kill a Mockingbird adaptations including the CBS drama; Chuck McGill conceals his electromagnetic hypersensitivity as a fabricated condition masking his true psychological instability and jealousy of Jimmy — each tile represents a character whose publicly known identity conceals a hidden, truer self across TV drama. NOTE: Saul Goodman and Chuck McGill both appear in Better Call Saul, creating a deliberate red herring suggesting a same_show grouping, but the real connection is secret/hidden identity across shows.
